title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Decision Notebook: Visualization
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int visualization increment in a tested numerical Python notebook and module. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose visual encodings, scales, labels, and uncertainty displays that support the question.
Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Visualization evidence
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (4 × 7) − (1 × 2).
